summary: Implemented M2 route transport state and read-model route preview. Runtime M2 boot now preserves routeKind, explicit baseTravelCost/baseCapacity, district seasonal curve assignments, and monthly seasonal values under state.m2.transport. Added deterministic integer seasonal route evaluation with stable tie-breakers, road/river seasonal route choice tests, capacity/duplicate-edge/order invariants, and protocol query parsing for sim.preview-m2-transport-route.

tests_run.result: Reported implementation changes plus pre-existing lead-owned orchestration changes in project/goal-mode-state.json, project/model-routing-state.json, project/tasks/active/M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001.json, project/tasks/thread-registry.json, and project/messages/routes/M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001__ROUTE-20260624-LEAD-TO-SIMULATION.json.
9 test files passed, 45 tests passed, including tests/sim-core-m2-route-transport.test.ts.
Node/worker-compatible hello, command/query, and save/load hashes matched. Command/query hash d275faa4; save/load hash 28074edd.
Architecture boundaries passed, determinism passed, root Vitest 16 files/79 tests passed, web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

tests_run.command: Scenario tests proving seasonal road/river route choice changes and route-capacity invariants
tests_run.result: Covered by tests/sim-core-m2-route-transport.test.ts via pnpm --filter @monsoon/sim-core test.

tests_run.command: node -e "const fs=require('node:fs'); const p='project/tasks/active/M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001.json'; const task=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p,'utf8')); const required=['id','summary','owner_role','reviewer_role','status','scope','acceptance_criteria','required_tests','risk_level','branch_or_worktree']; for (const key of required) { if (!(key in task)) throw new Error('missing '+key); } if (task.id !== 'M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001') throw new Error('bad id'); if (!Array.isArray(task.scope.allowed_paths) || !Array.isArray(task.scope.forbidden_paths)) throw new Error('bad scope'); console.log('M2-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-001 task JSON shape ok');"

tests_run.result: format:check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture:check passed.

decisions: Treated this as R3-sensitive because it changes WorldState canonical M2 state and protocol query surface; routed to systems_architect.
Kept transport inside state.m2 and did not touch packages/save-format, preserving the known non-blocking M2 save/load omission risk.
Kept RouteDefinition backward-compatible and stored M2 transport metadata under state.m2.transport to avoid save-format/client changes.
Used integer-only seasonal cost/capacity formulas and deterministic path tie-breakers: total cost, edge count, route ID sequence, direction, then destination district ID.
Coast routes are retained in explicit transport state but priced as high-cost fallback so the M2 acceptance scenario demonstrates road versus river seasonal choice.
risks: Canonical M2 boot state hash changed from d280a188 to 92903cba because state.m2.transport is now included in authoritative hash text.
Transport preview is read-only and does not implement actual stock transfer commands; this matches task scope but leaves committed stock movement for a later task.
M2 save/load still omits state.m2 per the known follow-up risk and was intentionally not changed.
requested_action: Review R3 WorldState/protocol route transport implementation, deterministic route tie-breakers, seasonal cost/capacity formulas, and read-model query boundary.
